body.u: “What a time I had”, I said out loud as I was thinking. My mind was wondering. It was one of those days. Then suddenly my mind flashed back, I must have been day dreaming. I suddenly went back in time to the last year of my school days. Things came clearer it was the last day of school, I remember going round the teachers to get my leaving form sighnedsigned up, I went round the school time after time.
Come four o’clock I was almost breathless.

Then as I walked along the footpath to the gate, I felt. This is it there was no turning back now, so I just kept on walking, the path seemed longer than usual, I surposesuppose it was because I knew it would probably be the last time I walked that path. As soon as I walked out of the gate I felt free.
My mind flashed on in time to my first day at work. It took quite some time to get a job and I was very nerversenervous.

That first day must have been the best of my life I made new friends met teachers and had a great day with my partner. My mind flashed again to when I met Stephanie my wife she was a nice young girl a year older than me, a big girl if you’ll pardon the expression. We got married and {tired?} peacefully just me and her till she got to the age of 47 she had been ill with a rare stomach diseacedisease ever scincesince she was a child and was in great pain, I spent every hour with I could, in hospital till she recovered and now we have both retired her from nursing me from technical work in the labs we have two fine children one a boy called john who was twenty theethree and a girl called Kim who was twenty five. They both have familysfamilies and are always good to me and Stephanie. Yes I must say we made a go at our lives and it paid ofoff well.
“Hmm” I sighed as I nodded ofoff to sleep hand in hand with Stephanie.
